title: Explicit undercut-window and traffic-window opportunity features
summary: Official F1 analysis says undercut strength depends on degradation, track position, overtaking difficulty, and being within striking range, while pit-loss variations can open windows earlier.
rationale: Official F1 analysis says undercut strength depends on degradation, track position, overtaking difficulty, and being within striking range, while pit-loss variations can open windows earlier. Your best solutions already model tyre feasibility, but not the tactical opportunity to emerge ahead of nearby cars if pitting now.
implementation_hint: Within each `(Race, Year, LapNumber)`, create relative features against nearby position rivals: tyre-life gap, degradation gap, lap-time-delta gap, and whether the car is inside an empirical undercut range to the car ahead/behind. Add lagged race-lap pit activity and a proxy for clear-air opportunity using position plus recent field pit rates. Keep all features current-lap or past-only.
expected_effect: Adds the missing race-relative decision layer for why a team pits now rather than one lap later, especially in midfield traffic.
risk: No true time-gap column exists, so proxies may be weak and can overfit specific races if too granular.
